case_name: Sagora Senior Living, Inc., WTCG Granbury Campus, LLC D/B/A Waterview - the Cove, and Waterview the Cove Assisted Living and Memory Care v. Andrea Wood and Jamie Jackson

summary.brief: Sagora Senior Living, Inc., WTCG Granbury Campus, LLC D/B/A Waterview - the Cove, and Waterview the Cove Assisted Living and Memory Care v. Andrea Wood and Jamie Jackson. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Appellees’ mother Carla Strasner, who was living in one of Appellants’ assisted-living facilities at the time of her death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In the fall of 2021, Strasner—a 59-year-old woman with a medical history of traumatic brain injury—lived in an assisted-living facility owned by Appellants.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Strasner had been prescribed and was taking two prescription medications with warnings against alcohol use.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At approximately 6:40 p.m. on October 1, 2021, a nurse discovered Strasner facedown and unconscious in her apartment at the assisted-living facility.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Emergency medical services arrived and resuscitated Strasner before transporting her to Lake Granbury Medical Center.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Strasner arrived at Lake Granbury Medical Center with diffuse pneumonia of the lungs, most likely caused by aspiration.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Her immediate cause of death was aspiration pneumonia, and her “sequential underlying cause of death” was alcohol intoxication.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: It is my opinion, based on reasonable medical probability, that more likely than not the failures outlined here proximately caused Carla Strasner’s death.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Lige Rushing

evidence_index.text: The standard of care for any long-term care facility, assisted living facility, or home health care require[s] that they neither accept nor retain a resident whose needs they are unable to meet.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Lige Rushing

evidence_index.text: The negligence of the facility and staff during supervising Carla Strasner during her stay is a proximate cause of Carla Strasner’s death.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Lige Rushing

evidence_index.text: We therefore affirm the trial court’s order.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient with a history of traumatic brain injury was found facedown and unconscious in her apartment at the assisted-living facility.

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient found unconscious at assisted-living facility

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Medical Response
timeline_phases.description: EMS resuscitation and transport to hospital

key_events.event: EMS resuscitated patient and transported to hospital

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient found unconscious with a history of traumatic brain injury and alcohol consumption
decision_points.prompt: What immediate action should be taken to ensure patient safety?

options.description: Provide close supervision and monitor alcohol consumption
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for patients with CNS depressants and alcohol risk
Neglects the risk of alcohol interaction with medications

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant allowed unsupervised access to alcohol
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Facility failed to provide adequate supervision

expected_action_court.description: Close supervision and monitoring should have been provided
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established need for supervision

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to supervise led to alcohol intoxication and aspiration

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to supervise alcohol consumption
Causation established - supervision could have prevented death

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Supervision could have prevented aspiration and death
